methodsAttempted.query: site:ku.edu University of Kansas generative AI data privacy IT guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search found official KU AI data-use, researcher, instructor, and CTE pages.
Followed central AI site links to GenAI guidelines, data use and protection, responsible AI, students, instructors, and researchers pages.
The linked approved-AI-tools path was checked and returned an official 404 page, so it was rejected as stale/inaccessible.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found a central University of Kansas AI guidance site and KU Center for Teaching Excellence AI guidance. The strongest evidence states KU does not currently have a university-wide GenAI teaching-and-learning policy, while existing academic integrity expectations and course-specific instructor policies continue to apply. Data handling and research guidance is present on official KU AI pages. A linked approved-AI-tools path was checked but rejected because it returned an official 404 page.

targets.sourceTitle: Building AI policies into your syllabus
Maintaining academic integrity in the AI era

stopConditions: Stop after official KU AI and CTE pages support narrow claim candidates.
Do not infer a binding university-wide GenAI teaching policy from CTE examples or instructor guidance.
Record stale, generic, or inaccessible official sources separately as source_rejection artifacts.

Be attentive to how AI tools handle data, including confidentiality, privacy, and intellectual property considerations.

artifacts.notes: Approved as a source-status claim because two official KU pages explicitly state there is no current university-wide GenAI teaching-and-learning policy.
Approved with narrow wording limited to course-specific expectations and student responsibility.
Approved as official GenAI guidance; wording avoids overclaiming a separate formal policy.
Approved as a narrow public-third-party-AI data-use claim backed by official KU data-protection guidance.
Approved with conditions limited to KU's stated Copilot sign-in, Enterprise Data Protection, and restricted-data consultation requirements.
Approved as research guidance tied to sponsor, publisher, institutional, disclosure, and data-handling language.
Approved as instructor-facing teaching guidance; not treated as a student conduct rule.
Approved as source-status/governance context because the responsible-AI page says existing activity- and data-based policies govern AI use.

artifacts.title: University of Kansas OpenClaw AI Policy Source Review
artifacts.summary: Official KU AI sources indicate there is no current university-wide policy specific to GenAI use in teaching and learning. KU nevertheless provides central GenAI guidance, student and instructor guidance, data-use rules for public and enterprise AI tools, researcher guidance, and responsible-AI governance context. Claims in this bundle are staged as agent-reviewed candidates and should not be treated as canonical until human or deterministic release review.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
The CTE syllabus and academic-integrity pages are treated as teaching guidance and examples, not as a binding university-wide GenAI policy.
The linked approved-AI-tools page returned an official 404 response during this crawl and was rejected as stale or inaccessible.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
